title: Content/lesson + glossary JSON Schema (sources[], reviewer sign-off, lastVerified/validUntil, jurisdiction, license)
context: Every lesson and glossary entry in the curriculum must carry structured provenance metadata: citations to authoritative open sources with reuse terms verified, a reviewer sign-off scoped to the current version, staleness fields (lastVerified/validUntil), jurisdiction labeling, and an explicit not-advice framing flag. A JSON Schema enforces these requirements at authoring time, preventing any lesson from shipping without a source or current-version sign-off. This schema is the machine-readable contract that the staleness check (task 009) and CI validate against.
objective: Define and publish a JSON Schema for lesson and glossary frontmatter that requires sources[], a current-version reviewer sign-off, lastVerified/validUntil staleness fields, jurisdiction labeling, readingLevel, and notAdviceFraming:true — and fails validation for any lesson missing a source or sign-off.
acceptanceCriteria: Schema requires sources[] with at minimum: citation, url, license/legal-status note, lastVerified (ISO date), and validUntil (ISO date) per source
Schema requires reviewer, reviewedVersion, and signoffDate fields; a lesson without a current-version sign-off fails schema validation
Schema requires jurisdiction field with 'neutral' or a valid ISO country code, and readingLevel, plainLanguageChecked, a11yChecked, and notAdviceFraming:true
Schema requires license field (MIT for code, CC-BY-4.0 or CC-BY-SA-4.0 for content); CC-BY-SA sources are tagged for inheritance
Schema validation runs in CI via `pnpm build` or a dedicated `pnpm validate:schema` script
Example valid and invalid frontmatter fixtures are included and tested
Schema is documented in schema/README or inline with comments explaining each required field
output: A merged PR adding the content/lesson and glossary JSON Schema (schema/lesson.schema.json and schema/glossary.schema.json) with CI validation, fixture tests, and documentation.
